claim_boundary.next_gate: Use the route-exposure family ledger to formulate topology-specific constraints without reusing R130 diagnostic seeds for acceptance.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: Causal hardware claims, new selector acceptance, verifier holdout performance, readout mitigation, current calibration, provider access, hardware execution, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or new B10 credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Post-hoc compiled route-exposure family attribution with selected-QASM and exposure-delta replay against R130.

requirements.label: R130 source is hash-bound and its exact seed block is reused
process hash seed is fixed and both layouts are recompiled for all rows
all selected recompilations byte-match stored R130 QASM
all exposure deltas match the R130 ledger
route-exposure families bind CX edge multisets and compiled exposure
every group has a complete ten-seed route-family ledger
all 60 automatic-layout QASM observations are frozen
diagnostic reuse opens no new seed block and performs no selection
verifier holdout, mitigation, current calibration, and hardware remain excluded
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new credit is claimed

title: B4/B8 R131 compiled route-exposure family attribution
